word_list.word: better a dinner of herbs than a stalled ox where hate is
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚɹ ɐ dˈɪnɚɹ ʌv ˈɜːbz ðˌænə stˈɔːld ˈɑːks wˌɛɹ hˈeɪt ɪz

senses.definition: used to emphasize the value of genuine connections and meaningful interactions over superficial or extravagant experiences
used to imply that good company can enhance the enjoyment of a meal or gathering, emphasizing the value of social connections and relationships in creating a positive atmosphere

word_list.pronunciation: ædvˈɚsɪɾi ɔːɹ pˈɑːlətˌɪks mˌeɪks stɹˈeɪndʒ bˈɛdfɪlˌoʊz

senses.definition: used to imply difficult situations can bring together unlikely allies, as people may put aside their differences to work toward a common goal in the face of adversity

examples.x: The man had always been at odds with his neighbor, but when a natural disaster struck their community, they both realized that adversity makes strange bedfellows, working together to help their neighbors and rebuild their community.

word_list.pronunciation: ˈæbsəns ɔːɹ dˈɪstəns mˌeɪks ðə hˈɑːɹt ɡɹˈoʊ fˈɑːndɚ

senses.definition: used to suggest that being separated from someone or something can increase one's affection and appreciation for them

examples.x: The woman had been away from her family for several months and realized that absence makes the heart grow fonder, as she felt a stronger emotional attachment to them upon her return.

word_list.word: better be alone than in bad company
word_list.pronunciation: bˈɛɾɚ biː ɐlˈoʊn ðɐn ɪn bˈæd kˈʌmpəni

senses.definition: used to emphasize the importance of choosing one's companions carefully and avoiding negative influences, even if it means being alone

examples.x: When Jane realized that her friends were involved in illegal activities, she decided to distance herself from them, remembering the saying better be alone than in bad company

word_list.word: clothing is cherishable when new, but relationship when old
word_list.pronunciation: klˈoʊðɪŋ ɪz tʃˈɛɹɪʃəbəl wɛn nˈuː bˌʌt ɹɪlˈeɪʃənʃˌɪp wɛn ˈoʊld

senses.definition: used to imply that while new things may bring temporary joy, long-lasting relationships are more valuable and worthy of cherishing
used to suggest that the more familiar one becomes with someone or something, the more likely one is to develop a negative attitude or lack of respect toward them

examples.x: Mark had always admired his boss, but after working for him for several years, he began to see his boss's weaknesses, and he started to develop a sense of contempt towards him, proving that familiarity breeds contempt.

word_list.word: good company on the road is the shortest cut
word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈʊd kˈʌmpəni ɑːnðə ɹˈoʊd ɪz ðə ʃˈɔːɹɾəst kˈʌt

senses.definition: used to imply that having good companionship on a journey can make the journey seem shorter and more enjoyable

examples.x: I was dreading the long road trip, but having my best friend with me made it much more enjoyable; after all, good company on the road is the shortest cut.

senses.definition: used to imply that when someone is away for a long time, people tend to forget about them or lose interest in them

word_list.word: out of sight, out of mind

senses.definition: used to imply that people tend to forget about things or people that are no longer visible or present in their lives

word_list.word: far from eye, far from heart
word_list.pronunciation: fˈɑːɹ fɹʌm ˈaɪ fˈɑːɹ fɹʌm hˈɑːɹt

senses.definition: used to suggest that when people or things are physically distant, there is a tendency for individuals to forget about them and feel less emotionally attached

word_list.word: they are not dead who live in the hearts they leave behind
word_list.pronunciation: ðeɪ ɑːɹ nˌɑːt dˈɛd hˌuː lˈɪv ɪnðə hˈɑːɹts ðeɪ lˈiːv bɪhˈaɪnd

senses.definition: used to imply that people who have passed away continue to live on through the memories and love they leave behind in the hearts of those who knew them

word_list.word: the blood of the covenant is thicker than the water of the womb
word_list.pronunciation: ðə blˈʌd ʌvðə kˈʌvənənt ɪz θˈɪkɚ ðɐn ðə wˈɔːɾɚɹ ʌvðə wˈuːm

senses.definition: used to suggest that chosen relationships based on shared experiences and mutual loyalty are more important than those based solely on blood ties that are not chosen
